Identification.LongTitle: An Act to enact the Digital Safety Act and the Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF CANADIAN IDENTITY AND CULTURE AND MINISTER RESPONSIBLE FOR OFFICIAL LANGUAGESâ

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act to enact the Digital Safety Act and the Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act and to make consequential amendments to other Actsâ.

Part 1 enacts the Digital Safety Act, whose purpose is, among other things, to promote the safety of persons in Canada, particularly children, reduce harms caused to them as a result of harmful content online and ensure that the operators of regulated social media services, regulated chatbot services and other regulated online services are transparent and accountable with respect to their duties under that Act.

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: imposes on the operators of regulated services

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: a duty to act responsibly in respect of the regulated social media services that they operate, including by implementing measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that users will be exposed to harmful content on the services, by providing tools to users to enable them to block other users and flag harmful content and by labelling synthetic content,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: a duty to act responsibly in respect of the regulated chatbot services that they operate, including by implementing measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that the chatbot will communicate harmful content to a user, measures to ensure intervention in crisis situations and measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that the chatbot will engage in certain harmful behaviours,

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: a duty to protect children in respect of all of the regulated services that they operate by integrating into those services design features that are set out in the regulations, by implementing minimum-age restrictions for accessing pornographic content on regulated services, and, if provided for by regulations, by implementing minimum-age restrictions for being able to have an account with, or being otherwise registered with, regulated social media services,

Provision.Label: (iv)
Provision.Text: a duty, in respect of the regulated social media services that they operate, to make inaccessible to persons in Canada, in certain circumstances, content on that service that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor and intimate content communicated without consent, and

Provision.Label: (v)
Provision.Text: a duty to be transparent in respect of all of the regulated services that they operate, including by submitting a digital safety plan to the Digital Safety Commission of Canada to assist it in determining whether an operator is complying with its duties under that Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorizes the Governor in Council to make regulations to make the operators of other online services subject to the duty to protect children and the duty to be transparent;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: provides that persons in Canada may make a complaint to the Digital Safety Commission of Canada that content on a regulated social media service is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent and authorizes the Commission to make an order requiring the operator of the service to make that content inaccessible to persons in Canada if the person making the complaint has used their best efforts to seek recourse from the operator of the service under that Act; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provides that persons in Canada may make submissions to the Digital Safety Commission of Canada respecting the measures taken by the operator of a regulated service to comply with its duties under that Act.

Part 2 enacts the Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act, which establishes the Digital Safety Commission of Canada, which has powers, duties and functions under the Digital Safety Act. The Part also makes consequential amendments to other Acts.

Chief executive officer and deputy head

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Safe Social Media Act.
The Digital Safety Act is enacted as follows:
This Act may be cited as the Digital Safety Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: act of terrorism or violent extremismâmeans an act of physical violence against a person, or an act that causes property damage, that is committed for a political, religious or ideological purpose, with the intention of intimidating the public or any segment of the public, of compelling a person, government or domestic or international organization to do or to refrain from doing any act or of undermining,â¯weakeningâ¯or destroying fundamental institutions or political,â¯economicâ¯or social stability, and that could cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: serious bodily harm to a person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a personâs life to be endangered; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a serious risk to the health or safety of the public or any segment of the public.â(acte de terrorisme ou dâextrÃ©misme violent)

Definition.Text: category of online servicesâmeans a category of online services established by regulations made under section 5.â(catÃ©gorie de services en ligne)
chatbot serviceâmeans an artificial intelligence system that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: communicates over the Internet;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is made available on a website or through an application that is publicly accessible in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: uses a natural language interface to provide, in a conversational format, adaptive, human-like responses to user inputs;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is capable of being used, by means of multiple interactions or sessions, to simulate a sustained human-like relationship with a user, including one that may resemble friendship, an intimate relationship or therapeutic support; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: generates content or responses that are not fully predetermined by the developer of the system or the person that operates the system.â(service dâagent conversationnel)

Definition.Text: childâmeans a person who is under 18 years of age.â(enfant)
Commissionâmeans the Digital Safety Commission of Canada established by section 4 of the Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act.â(Commission)

content that foments hatredâmeans content that expresses detestation or vilification of an individual or group of individuals on the basis of a prohibited ground of discrimination, within the meaning of the Canadian Human Rights Act, and that, given the context in which it is communicated, is likely to foment detestation or vilification of an individual or group of individuals on the basis of such a prohibited ground.â(contenu fomentant la haine)

content that incites violenceâmeans content that actively encourages a person to commit â or that actively threatens the commission of â an act of physical violence against a person or an act that causes property damage, and that, given the context in which it is communicated, could cause a person to commit an act that could cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: serious bodily harm to a person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a personâs life to be endangered; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: serious interference with or serious disruption of an essential service, facility or system.â(contenu incitant Ã la violence)

Definition.Text: content that induces a child to harm themselvesâmeans content that advocates self-harm, disordered eating or dying by suicide or that counsels a person to commit or engage in any of those acts, and that, given the context in which it is communicated, could cause a child to inflict injury on themselves, to have an eating disorder or to die by suicide.â(contenu poussant un enfant Ã se porter prÃ©judice)
content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivorâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation that shows a child, or a person depicted as being a child, who is engaged in or depicted as being engaged in explicit sexual activity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation that depicts the sexual organs or anal region of a child, if it is reasonable to suspect that the representation is created or communicated for a sexual purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: written material or an audio recording whose dominant characteristic is the description, presentation or representation of explicit sexual activity with a child, if it is reasonable to suspect that the material or recording is created or communicated for a sexual purpose;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation, written material or an audio recording that shows, describes, presents or represents any of the following, if it is reasonable to suspect that the representation, material or recording is created or communicated for a sexual purpose:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person touching, in a sexual manner, directly or indirectly, with a part of their body or with an object, any part of the body of a child or a person depicted as being a child,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person who is engaged in or depicted as being engaged in explicit sexual activity in the presence of a child or a person depicted as being a child, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person exposing their sexual organs or anal region in the presence of a child or a person depicted as being a child;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation, written material or an audio recording in which or by means of which sexual activity between a person who is 18 years of age or more and a child is advocated, counselled or planned, other than one in which or by means of which sexual activity between a person who is 16 years of age or more but under 18 years of age and another person who is less than two years older than that person is advocated, counselled or planned;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation that shows a child who is being subjected to cruel, inhuman or degrading acts of physical violence, whether the acts are sexual in nature or not;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any excerpt of a visual representation referred to in paragraph (a), if it is reasonable to suspect that the communication of the excerpt perpetuates harm against a person who as a child appeared in the visual representation; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation, written material or an audio recording that, given the context in which it is communicated, is likely to bring to light a connection between a person and a visual representation, written material or audio recording referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (d) in which the person appeared as a child, if it is reasonable to suspect that the communication of the representation, material or recording that is likely to bring to light that connection perpetuates harm against the person.â(contenu reprÃ©sentant de la victimisation sexuelle dâenfants ou perpÃ©tuant la victimisation de survivants)

Definition.Text: content used to bully a childâmeans content, or an aggregate of content, that, given the context in which it is communicated, could cause serious harm to a childâs physical or mental health, if it is reasonable to suspect that the content or the aggregate of content is communicated for the purpose of threatening, intimidating or humiliating the child.â(contenu visant Ã intimider un enfant)
harmful contentâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: intimate content communicated without consent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: content that induces a child to harm themselves;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: content used to bully a child;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: content that foments hatred;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: content that incites violence; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: terrorism or violent extremism content.â(contenu prÃ©judiciable)

Definition.Text: Indigenous peoples of Canadaâhas the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.â(peuples autochtones du Canada)
intimate content communicated without consentâmeans

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a visual recording, such as a photographic, film or video recording, in which a person is nude, is nearly nude or is exposing their sexual organs or anal region or is engaged in explicit sexual activity, if it is reasonable to suspect that the person

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: had a reasonable expectation of privacy at the time of the recording, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: does not consent to the recording being communicated; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation that is made by any electronic or mechanical means, including by means of artificial intelligence software, and that shows an identifiable person who is depicted as nude, as nearly nude, as exposing their sexual organs or anal region or as engaged in explicit sexual activity, if the depiction is likely to be mistaken for a visual recording of that person and if it is reasonable to suspect that the person does not consent to the representation being communicated.â(contenu intime communiquÃ© de faÃ§on non consensuelle)

Definition.Text: Ministerâmeans the member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada designated under section 13 or, if no member is designated, the Minister of Canadian Heritage.ââ(ministre)

online serviceâmeans a website or application, other than a social media service or a chatbot service, that is accessible in Canada over the Internet and that allows users of the website or application to interact with the website or application.â(service en ligne)
operatorâmeans a person that, through any means, operates a regulated service.â(exploitant)
personâincludes a corporation and a trust, a partnership, a fund, a joint venture and any other unincorporated association or organization.â(personne)
personal informationâhas the same meaning as in section 3 of the Privacy Act.â(renseignements personnels)

pornographic contentâmeans a visual representation the dominant characteristic of which is the depiction, for a sexual purpose, of a personâs genital organs or anal region.â(contenu pornographique)
regulated chatbot serviceâmeans a chatbot service referred to in subsection 7(1).â(service dâagent conversationnel rÃ©glementÃ©)
regulated online serviceâmeans an online service referred to in subsection 8(1).â(service en ligne rÃ©glementÃ©)
regulated serviceâmeans a regulated social media service, a regulated chatbot service or a regulated online service.â(service rÃ©glementÃ©)
regulated social media serviceâmeans a social media service referred to in subsection 6(1).â(service de mÃ©dia social rÃ©glementÃ©)

social media serviceâmeans a website or application that is accessible in Canada, the primary purpose of which is to facilitate interprovincial or international online communication among users of the website or application by enabling them to access and share content.â(service de mÃ©dia social)

synthetic contentâmeans an audio representation or visual representation of a person, object, place, entity or event that is made by electronic or mechanical means, including by means of artificial intelligence software, if the depiction is likely to be mistaken for an authentic audio or visual recording of a person, object, place, entity or event.â(contenu synthÃ©tique)
terrorism or violent extremism contentâmeans content that, given the context in which it is communicated,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: actively encourages, instructs or counsels a person to facilitate or commit an act of terrorism or violent extremism;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: threatens or facilitates the commission of an act of terrorism or violent extremism;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: aims to recruit a person to facilitate or commit acts of terrorism or violent extremism;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: offers or provides training,â¯skillsâ¯orâ¯expertiseâ¯to a person so that they or another person may facilitateâ¯or commit an act of terrorism or violent extremism;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: actively encourages a person to collect, provide or makeâ¯available a material benefit, including a financial benefit, to facilitate or commit an act of terrorism or violent extremism; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: promotes an act of terrorism or violent extremism in a manner that could reasonably be expected to encourage others to facilitate or commit acts of terrorism or violent extremism.â(contenu de terrorisme ou dâextrÃ©misme violent)

Definition.Text: visual representationâincludes a visual recording such as a photographic, film or video recording.â(reprÃ©sentation visuelle)

Subsection.MarginalNote: For greater certainty â social media service
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, a social media service includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an adult content service, namely a social media service that is focused on enabling its users to access and share pornographic content; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a live streaming service, namely a social media service that is focused on enabling its users to access and share content by live stream.

Subsection.MarginalNote: For greater certainty â content that foments hatred
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty and for the purposes of the definition content that foments hatred, content does not express detestation or vilification solely because it expresses disdain or dislike or it discredits, humiliates, hurts or offends.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exclusion â content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor
Subsection.Text: The definition content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor does not include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation referred to in paragraph (a) of that definition whose creation and communication have a legitimate purpose related to the administration of justice or to science, medicine, education or art and do not pose an undue risk of harm to children;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation referred to in paragraph (f) of that definition whose creation or communication has a legitimate purpose referred to in paragraph (a) and does not pose an undue risk of harm to children;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an excerpt of a visual representation referred to in paragraph (g) of that definition that is communicated for a legitimate purpose referred to in paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a visual representation, written material or an audio recording referred to in paragraph (h) of that definition that is communicated for a legitimate purpose referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exclusion â act of terrorism or violent extremism
Subsection.Text: The definition act of terrorism or violent extremism does not include activities undertaken by military forces of a state in the exercise of their official duties.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exclusion â terrorism or violent extremism content
Subsection.Text: The definition terrorism or violent extremism content does not include content whose communication has a legitimate purpose, including a legitimate purpose related to the administration of justice or to journalism, education or art.
The definition chatbot service does not include an artificial intelligence system that exclusively serves a purpose specified in the regulations.
The definition online service does not include a website or application

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whose primary purpose is to facilitate the sale, listing or advertisement of goods or services; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whose primary purpose is to provide directories, search results, maps or navigation tools.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, if the operator is a telecommunications service provider, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Telecommunications Act, the duties imposed under this Act on the operator do not apply in respect of any service that the operator provides that provides basic connectivity to the Internet.

Section.MarginalNote: Gross global revenue â affiliated groups of persons

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph 88(a) and subparagraphs 107(2)(a)(i) and (b)(i) and 108(2)(a)(i) and (b)(i), if a person is part of a group of persons that are affiliated, the personâs gross global revenue, for the financial year before the one in which the penalty or fine, as the case may be, is imposed, is deemed to be the gross global revenue of the group for that financial year.

For the purposes of subsection (1), a person is affiliated with another person if one of them is wholly owned by the other, if both are wholly owned by the same person or if their financial statements are consolidated.

Section.MarginalNote: Regulations â categories of online services

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Commission, make regulations establishing one or more categories of online services if the Governor in Council is satisfied that online services within the category being established pose a significant risk of harm to children in Canada.
The Commission must not make a recommendation referred to in subsection (1) unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it has, at the direction of the Minister, undertaken an analysis to determine if the online services specified by the Minister pose a significant risk of harm to children in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it has provided to the Governor in Council a report that sets out the results of its analysis; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the report indicates that the Commission is satisfied that the online services pose a significant risk of harm to children in Canada.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a regulated social media service is a social media service that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has a number of users that is equal to or greater than the significant number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection (2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has a number of users that is less than the number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection (2) and is designated as a regulated social media service by regulations made under subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing types of social media services;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the number of users referred to in that subsection, for each type of social media service; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of determining the number of users of a social media service.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1)(b), the Governor in Council may make regulations designating a particular social media service as a regulated social media service if the Governor in Council is satisfied that there is a significant risk that harmful content is accessible on the service.

At the Ministerâs request and within the time and in the manner specified by the Minister, the Commission must provide to the Minister any information that is relevant for the purposes of subsection (3).
For the purposes of this Act, a regulated chatbot service is a chatbot service that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has a number of users that is equal to or greater than the significant number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection (2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has a number of users that is less than the number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection (2) and is designated as a regulated chatbot service by regulations made under subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing types of chatbot services;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the number of users referred to in that subsection, for each type of chatbot service; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of determining the number of users of a chatbot service.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1)(b), the Governor in Council may make regulations designating a particular chatbot service as a regulated chatbot service if the Governor in Council is satisfied that the service poses a significant risk of harm to individuals in Canada.

At the Ministerâs request and within the time and in the manner specified by the Minister, the Commission must provide to the Minister any information that is relevant for the purposes of subsection (3).
For the purposes of this Act, a regulated online service is an online service that is within a category of online services and that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has a number of users that is equal to or greater than the significant number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection (2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has a number of users that is less than the number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection (2) and is designated as a regulated online service by regulations made under subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establishing types of online services that are within a category of online services;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the number of users referred to in that subsection, for each type of online service; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner of determining the number of users of an online service that is within a category of online services.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1)(b), the Governor in Council may make regulations designating a particular online service that is within a category of online services as a regulated online service if the Governor in Council is satisfied that the service poses a significant risk of harm to children in Canada.

At the Ministerâs request and within the time and in the manner specified by the Minister, the Commission must provide to the Minister any information that is relevant for the purposes of subsection (3).

Section.Text: At the Commissionâs request and within the time and in the manner specified by it, the person that, through any means, operates a social media service, a chatbot service or an online service that is within a category of online services must provide to the Commission any information provided for by regulations, for the purpose of enabling the Commission to determine whether that social media service, chatbot service or online service has a number of users that is equal to or greater than the number of users provided for by regulations made under subsection 6(2), 7(2) or 8(2), respectively.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a service is not a social media service if it does not enable a user to communicate content to the public.

For the purposes of subsection (1), a service does not enable a user to communicate content to the public if it does not enable the user to communicate content to a potentially unlimited number of users not determined by the user.
The duties imposed under this Act on the operator of a regulated social media service or a regulated online service do not apply in respect of any private messaging feature of the service.
For the purposes of subsection (1), private messaging feature means a feature that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enables a user to communicate content to a limited number of users determined by the user; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: does not enable a user to communicate content to a potentially unlimited number of users not determined by the user.

Section.MarginalNote: Proactive search of content not required

Subsection.Text: Nothing in this Act requires an operator to proactively search content on a regulated service that it operates in order to identify harmful content.

Despite subsection (1), regulations made under paragraph 126(1)(i) may require the operator of a regulated service to use technological means to prevent content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor from being uploaded to the service.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada as the Minister for the purposes of this Act.
The purposes of this Act are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: promote the safety of persons in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: protect childrenâs physical and mental health;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: considering that exposure to harmful content online impacts the safety and well-being of persons in Canada, mitigate the risk that persons in Canada will be exposed to harmful content online while respecting their freedom of expression;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: enable persons in Canada to participate fully in public discourse and exercise their freedom of expression online without being hindered by harmful content;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: enable persons in Canada to benefit from the use of chatbot services while reducing the risk of harm to the users of those services and other persons;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: reduce harms caused to persons in Canada as a result of harmful content online;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: make content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor and intimate content communicated without consent inaccessible online;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that operators are transparent and accountable with respect to their duties under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: contribute to the development of standards with respect to online safety.

Section.Text: For the purposes of this Act, the Commissionâs mandate is to promote online safety in Canada and contribute to the reduction of harms caused to persons in Canada as a result of harmful content online by, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: ensuring the administration and enforcement of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensuring that operators are transparent and accountable with respect to their duties under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: investigating complaints relating to content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor and intimate content communicated without consent;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contributing to the development of standards with respect to online safety through research and educational activities;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: facilitating the participation of Indigenous peoples of Canada, official language minority communities and interested persons in the Commissionâs activities;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: collaborating with interested persons, including operators, the Commissionâs international counterparts and other persons having professional, technical or specialized knowledge;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: providing support to users of regulated services;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: advocating for the public interest with respect to systemic issues related to online safety;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: gathering information with respect to issues related to online safety, including with respect to harmful content, such as by obtaining the perspective of users of regulated services and victims of harmful content; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: directing users of regulated services to resources, including those provided for under this Act, that may address their concerns regarding online safety.

Section.Text: The Commission may issue guidelines, codes of conduct and other such documents for the purposes of this Act.
The Commission must issue guidelines setting out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the circumstances in which an operator of a regulated service should notify the Royal Canadian Mounted Police of the existence on the service of content that gives rise to reasonable grounds to suspect that there is a risk that an individual will commit an act that would cause death or serious bodily harm to another individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information that should be provided to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police in such circumstances; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which that information should be provided to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police as well as any other recommendations that the Commission considers appropriate to ensure that the Royal Canadian Mounted Police receives the information.

Section.Text: When making regulations and issuing guidelines, codes of conduct and other documents for the purposes of this Act, the Commission must take into account

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: freedom of expression;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: equality rights;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: privacy rights;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the needs and perspectives of the Indigenous peoples of Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any other factor that the Commission considers relevant.

Section.Text: The Commission must, on request of the Governor in Council, hold hearings, other than a hearing under section 75, or make reports, on any matter that is within the Commissionâs mandate under this Act.
An operator has a duty, in respect of every regulated service that it operates, to protect children by complying with sections 21 to 23.
An operator must integrate into every regulated service that it operates the design features respecting the protection of children set out in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: If an operator has reasonable grounds to suspect that a regulated service that they operate provides access to pornographic content, the operator must, in respect of that regulated service, implement age-verification or age-estimation measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that children will be exposed to pornographic content on the service.
In order to determine whether the measures implemented under subsection (1) are adequate, the Commission must be satisfied that the measures

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: do not involve the collection or use of personal information other than for age-verification or age-estimation purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the destruction of personal information collected for age-verification or age-estimation purposes once the verification or estimation is completed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the protection of personal information that is collected for age-verification or age-estimation purposes until that information is destroyed; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: comply with any other requirement specified in the regulations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No unreasonable or disproportionate limit on expression
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not require the operator to implement measures that unreasonably or disproportionately limit usersâ expression.

Section.Text: An operator of a regulated service must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations to mitigate the risk that children who use the service will be exposed to pornographic content on the service.
The Commission may establish guidelines respecting the protection of children in relation to regulated services.

Subject to the regulations, the operator of a regulated service must keep all records, including information and data, that are necessary to determine whether the operator is complying with its duties under this Act.
An operator has a duty to protect children in respect of a regulated social media service that it operates by complying with sections 27 and 28.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 29, an operator must, with respect to every regulated social media service specified by regulations made by the Governor in Council under subsection (5) that it operates, implement adequate age-verification or age-estimation measures designed to prevent a person under the age of 16 from being able to have an account with, or be otherwise registered with, the service.
In order to determine whether the measures implemented under subsection (1) are adequate, the Commission must be satisfied that the measures

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: do not involve the collection or use of personal information other than for age-verification or age-estimation purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the destruction of personal information that is collected for age-verification or age-estimation purposes once the verification or estimation is completed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide for the protection of personal information that is collected for age-verification or age-estimation purposes until that information is destroyed; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: comply with any other requirement specified in the regulations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No unreasonable or disproportionate limit on expression
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not require the operator to implement measures that unreasonably or disproportionately limit usersâ expression.

Subsection (1) applies only in respect of regulated social media services â or regulated social media services that fall within classes of regulated social media services â specified in regulations made under subsection (5).
The Governor in Council may make regulations specifying regulated social media services or classes of regulated social media services for the purposes of subsection (4).

Subject to section 29, an operator of a regulated social media service specified by regulations made by the Governor in Council under subsection (3) must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations that prevent persons under the age of 16 from being able to have an account with, or being otherwise registered with, the regulated social media service.

Subsection (1) applies only in respect of regulated social media services â or regulated social media services that fall within classes of regulated social media services â specified in regulations made under subsection (3).
The Governor in Council may make regulations specifying regulated social media services or classes of regulated social media services for the purposes of subsection (2).

The Commission may, on application by an operator, exempt, subject to any conditions it considers appropriate, the operator from the application of subsections 27(1) and 28(1) in respect of a regulated social media service if the Commission is satisfied that the operator provides adequate safeguards in the regulated social media service for the protection of children.

The Governor in Council may make regulations setting out criteria that the Commission must consider when making determinations as to whether adequate safeguards have been provided for the protection of children.

Section.Text: The Commission may establish guidelines respecting, for the purpose of section 29, what constitutes adequate safeguards in a regulated social media service for the protection of children.

An operator has a duty to act responsibly in respect of a regulated social media service that it operates by complying with sections 32 to 41 and with orders made by the Commission under subsection 61(1).

Section.MarginalNote: Duty to implement measures â harmful content

Subsection.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must implement measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that users of the service will be exposed to harmful content on the service.
In order to determine whether the measures implemented by the operator under subsection (1) are adequate, the Commission must take into account the following factors:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the effectiveness of the measures in mitigating the risk that users of the service will be exposed to harmful content on the service;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the size of the service, including the number of users;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the technical and financial capacity of the operator;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: whether the measures are designed or implemented in a manner that is discriminatory on the basis of a prohibited ground of discrimination within the meaning of the Canadian Human Rights Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any factor provided for by regulations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: No unreasonable or disproportionate limit on expression
Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not require the operator to implement measures that unreasonably or disproportionately limit usersâ expression.

Section.MarginalNote: Measures in regulations â harmful content
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations to mitigate the risk that users of the service will be exposed to harmful content on the service.
The operator of a regulated social media service must make user guidelines publicly available on the service. The user guidelines must be accessible and easy to use and must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a standard of conduct that applies to users with respect to harmful content; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the measures that the operator implements with respect to harmful content on the service.

Section.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must make available to users who have an account or are otherwise registered with the service tools that enable those users to block other users who have an account or are otherwise registered with the service from finding or communicating with them on the service.

Section.MarginalNote: Tools and processes to flag harmful content

Subsection.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must implement tools and processes to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enable a user of the service to easily flag to the operator content that is accessible on the service as being a particular type of harmful content;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notify a user of the service who flagged content as being a particular type of harmful content of the operatorâs receipt of the flag as well as of any measures taken by the operator with respect to the content or of the fact that no measures were taken; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: notify a user of the service who communicated content that was flagged as being a particular type of harmful content of the fact that the content was flagged as well as of any measures taken by the operator with respect to the content or of the fact that no measures were taken.

Subsection.Text: In notifying a user of any measures taken with respect to content in accordance with paragraph (1)(b) or (c), the operator must not notify a user of any report that the operator has made to a law enforcement agency in relation to the content.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty to implement measures â synthetic content

Subsection.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must, to the extent that it is reasonable for the operator to do so, implement adequate measures to label, as being synthetic content, any synthetic content accessible on the service that meets the criteria provided for by regulations.

In order to determine whether the measures implemented by the operator under subsection (1) are adequate, or whether it is reasonable for the operator to implement measures under that subsection, the Commission must take into account the following factors:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the effectiveness of the measures in labelling, as being synthetic content, any synthetic content that is accessible on the service;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the effectiveness of the measures in avoiding incorrectly labelling, as being synthetic content, any content that is accessible on the service;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the extent to which it is technically feasible to identify synthetic content that is accessible on the service;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the size of the service, including the number of users;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the technical and financial capacity of the operator; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any factor provided for by regulations.

Section.MarginalNote: Measures in regulations â synthetic content
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations in respect of the labelling of synthetic content as synthetic content.

Section.MarginalNote: Multiple instances of automated communication by computer program
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must label, as being content that is described in this section, harmful content â other than content that is referred to in subsections 43(1) and 44(1) â that is accessible on the service if the operator has reasonable grounds to believe that the content

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is the subject of multiple instances of automated communication on the service by a computer program, other than a computer program that is implemented by the operator to facilitate the proper functioning of the service; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is more prominent on the service than it would have been had it not been the subject of those multiple instances of automated communication by a computer program.

Subsection.Text: The operator of a regulated social media service must make a resource person available to users of the service to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: receive usersâ concerns with respect to harmful content on the service or with respect to the measures that the operator implements to comply with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: direct users to internal and external resources to address their concerns, such as an internal complaints mechanism, the Commission or a law enforcement agency; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide guidance to users with respect to those internal resources.

Subsection.Text: The operator must ensure that the resource person is easily identifiable and that their contact information is easily accessible to users of the service.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty to preserve certain harmful content

Subsection.Text: If the operator of a regulated social media service makes inaccessible to all persons in Canada content that incites violence or terrorism or violent extremism content, the operator must preserve that content, and all other computer data related to it that is in the operatorâs possession or control, for a period of one year beginning on the day on which the content is made inaccessible.

After the end of the one-year period, the operator must, as soon as feasible, destroy the content and any other computer data related to it that would not be retained in the ordinary course of business, as well as any document that was prepared for the purpose of preserving that content and data, unless the operator is required to preserve the content, data or document under a judicial order made under any other Act of Parliament or an Act of the legislature of a province or under a preservation demand made under section 487.012 of the Criminal Code.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception â content never made accessible
Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to content that was never made accessible on the regulated social media service.

In this section, computer data has the same meaning as in subsection 1(1) of An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service.

The operator of a regulated social media service must submit a digital safety plan to the Commission in respect of each regulated social media service that it operates. The digital safety plan must include the following information in respect of the period provided for in the regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with sections 32 and 33, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the risk that users of the service will be exposed to harmful content on the service,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the measures that the operator implements to mitigate the risk,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the effectiveness of the measures, both individually and collectively, in mitigating the risk,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the indicators that the operator uses to assess the effectiveness of the measures, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: information respecting the factors referred to in subsection 32(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with sections 34 to 40, including a description of the measures that the operator implements under those sections and information respecting the factors referred to in subsection 37(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with section 21, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the design features that the operator integrates into the service under that section,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the effectiveness of the measures, both individually and collectively, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the indicators that the operator uses to assess the effectiveness of the measures;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with subsections 22(1) and 27(1);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with the regulations made under paragraphs 126(1)(d) and (f);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting any measures that the operator implements to protect children, other than those that it implements to fulfill its obligations under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the criteria and processes, if any, that the operator applies to determine if the Royal Canadian Mounted Police â or another law enforcement agency â should be notified of the existence on the service of content that gives rise to reasonable grounds to suspect that there is a risk that an individual will commit an act that would cause death or serious bodily harm to another individual;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if any law enforcement agencies were notified of the existence on the service of content described in paragraph (g), information respecting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of such notifications,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the name of each law enforcement agency notified, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which each was notified;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the resources, including human resources, that the operator allocates in order to comply with sections 21 to 23, 27, 28 and 32 to 41, including information respecting the resources that the operator allocates to automated decision making;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the volume and type of harmful content that was accessible on the service, including the volume and type of harmful content that was moderated and the volume and type of harmful content that would have been accessible on the service had it not been moderated, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which and the time within which harmful content was moderated;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of times that content that was accessible on the service was flagged to the operator by users of the service as being harmful content, including the number of flags relating to each type of harmful content,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which the operator triaged and assessed the flags,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the measures taken by the operator with respect to content that was flagged as being harmful content, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the time within which the operator took those measures;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the content, other than harmful content, that was moderated by the operator and that the operator had reasonable grounds to believe posed a risk of significant psychological or physical harm, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the content,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the volume of the content that was accessible on the service or that would have been accessible had it not been moderated, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which and the time within which the content was moderated;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the concerns received by the resource person referred to in subsection 40(1) and the internal and external resources to which the resource person directed users for the purposes of that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the topics, and a summary of the findings, conclusions or recommendations, of any research conducted by or on behalf of the operator with respect to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: harmful content on the service,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: content on the service that poses a risk of significant psychological or physical harm, other than harmful content, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: design features of the service that pose a risk of significant psychological or physical harm;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the measures implemented by the operator for the purposes of complying with its duties with respect to the service under An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: an inventory of all electronic data, other than content that was communicated by users of the service, that was used to prepare the information referred to in paragraphs (a) to (k), (m) and (q); and

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: any other information provided for by regulations.

Subsection.Text: The operator must ensure that the digital safety plan does not contain any personal information.

For greater certainty, the digital safety plan must not contain any information that must not be disclosed under section 5 of An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service.
The operator must make the digital safety plan publicly available on the service to which the plan relates in an accessible and easy-to-read format.
The digital safety plan that the operator makes publicly available must not contain

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any information about the circumstances in which a law enforcement agency was notified about the existence on the operatorâs service of content described in paragraph (1)(g); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other information regarding the notification of a law enforcement agency about the existence of such content on the operatorâs service, if disclosure of the information could prejudice a criminal investigation, whether or not a criminal investigation has begun.

Subsection.Text: The operator is not required to include any of the following information in the digital safety plan that it makes publicly available:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the inventory of electronic data referred to in paragraph (1)(p);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that is a trade secret; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: financial, commercial, scientific or technical information that is confidential and that is treated consistently in a confidential manner by the person to whose business or affairs it relates.

Heading.TitleText: Duty to Make Certain Content Inaccessible

Subsection.Text: If the operator of a regulated social media service identifies on the service, other than as a result of a flag by a user, content that the operator has reasonable grounds to suspect is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent, the operator

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must make that content inaccessible to all persons in Canada within the period that applies under subsection (2) and continue to make it inaccessible until the operator makes a decision under subsection 45(2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must, within the period that applies under subsection (2), give notice to the user who communicated the content on the service that the content has been made inaccessible.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), the period is the period of 24 hours â or, if a period of a different length is provided for by regulations, the period of that length â after the operator identifies the content.

If a user of a regulated social media service flags to the operator content on the service as being content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent, the operator must, within the period that applies under subsection (5), conduct an initial assessment of the flag and dismiss the flag if the operator is of the opinion that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the flag is trivial, frivolous, vexatious or made in bad faith; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the content that is the subject of the flag is or has already been the subject of another flag and further assessment of the flag is unnecessary having regard to all of the circumstances.

Subsection.Text: If the operator dismisses the flag, it must, within the period that applies under subsection (5), notify the user who flagged the content of the dismissal.

If the operator does not dismiss the flag, the operator must make the content inaccessible to all persons in Canada within the period that applies under subsection (5) and continue to make it inaccessible until the operator makes a decision under subsection 45(2).

If the operator makes content inaccessible under subsection (3), the operator must, within the period that applies under subsection (5), give notice to the user who communicated the content on the service and the user who flagged the content that the content has been made inaccessible.

For the purposes of subsections (1) to (4), the period is the period of 24 hours â or, if a period of a different length is provided for by regulations, the period of that length â after the content is flagged by a user.

If the operator of a regulated social media service makes content inaccessible under paragraph 43(1)(a) or subsection 44(3), the operator must, within the period provided for by regulations, give the user who communicated the content on the service and the user who flagged the content, if applicable, an opportunity to make representations as to whether the content is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent.

As soon as feasible after the end of the period provided for by regulations for making representations or, if a different period is provided for by regulations, within that period, the operator must decide whether there are reasonable grounds to believe that the content that was made inaccessible to all persons in Canada is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent.
As soon as feasible after making a decision, the operator must give notice of the decision to the user who communicated the content on the service and the user who flagged the content, if applicable.

If the operator decides that there are reasonable grounds to believe that the content is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent, the operator must continue to make the content inaccessible to all persons in Canada.

At the request of the user who communicated the content on the service or the user who flagged the content, the operator of a regulated social media service must reconsider a decision made under subsection 45(2).

The operator must, within the period provided for by regulations, give the user who communicated the content on the service and the user who flagged the content, if applicable, an opportunity to make representations as to whether the content is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent.

As soon as feasible after the end of the period provided for by regulations for making representations or, if a different period is provided for by regulations, within that period, the operator must reconsider a decision made under subsection 45(2).

As soon as feasible after making a decision under subsection (3), the operator must give notice of the decision to the user who communicated the content on the service and the user who flagged the content, if applicable.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Duty to continue to make inaccessible ceases
Subsection.Text: If the operator decides, on reconsideration under subsection (3), that there are no reasonable grounds to believe that the content is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent, the operatorâs duty to continue to make the content inaccessible under subsection 45(4), if applicable, ceases.

If the operator decides, on reconsideration under subsection (3), that there are reasonable grounds to believe that the content is content that sexually victimizes a child or revictimizes a survivor or intimate content communicated without consent, the operator must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make the content inaccessible to all persons in Canada without delay, if subsection 45(4) does not already apply to the operator in respect of the content; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: continue to make the content inaccessible to all persons in Canada if subsection 45(4) applies.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in sections 43 to 46 affects any duty that the operator of a regulated social media service may have under An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service.
An operator has a duty to act responsibly in respect of a regulated chatbot service that it operates by complying with sections 49 to 57 and with orders made by the Commission under subsection 61(1).

Section.MarginalNote: Duty to implement measures â harmful content
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that the service will communicate harmful content to a user of the service.

Section.MarginalNote: Measures in regulations â harmful content
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations to mitigate the risk that the service will communicate harmful content to a user of the service.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty to implement emergency measures â crisis intervention
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement measures that, if a user of the service expresses, on the service, a suicidal ideation, an intention to self-harm or an intention to commit an act that could cause death or serious bodily harm to an individual, cause the service to immediately interrupt its interaction with the user in order to direct the user towards crises intervention services that are appropriate to the situation, are available at the moment when the user is directed towards them and permit the user to interact with a human being.

Section.MarginalNote: Measures in regulations â emergency situations
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations to address situations in which a user of the service expresses, on the service, a suicidal ideation, an intention to self-harm or an intention to commit an act that could cause death or serious bodily harm to an individual.

Section.MarginalNote: Duty to implement measures â harmful behaviour
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement measures that are adequate to mitigate the risk that the service will engage in any of the following types of harmful behaviour:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: posing as a human being in a manner likely to lead a user of the service to mistake it for a human being or otherwise being deceptive about being an artificial intelligence system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: posing as a medical, legal or other licensed professional and giving advice based on that deception that could reasonably be expected to be relied on by a user of the service;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: using manipulative engagement techniques to encourage a user of the service to form or maintain an emotional attachment to the service in a way that may encourage the user to withdraw socially or disconnect from reality;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: encouraging self-harm, suicide or the commission of acts that could cause death or serious bodily harm to an individual; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any other type of behaviour specified in the regulations.

Section.MarginalNote: Measures in regulations â harmful behaviour
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement any measures that are provided for by regulations to mitigate the risk that the service will engage in any type of harmful behaviour referred to in section 53.

The operator of a regulated chatbot service must make user guidelines publicly available on the service. The user guidelines must be accessible and easy to use and must include a description of the measures that the operator implements to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: mitigate the risk that the service will communicate harmful content;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: address situations in which a user of the service expresses, on the service, a suicidal ideation, an intention to self-harm or an intention to commit an act that could cause death or serious bodily harm to an individual; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: mitigate the risk that the service will engage in any type of harmful behaviour referred to in section 53.

Section.MarginalNote: Tools and processes to flag harmful content
Section.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must implement tools and processes to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enable a user of the service to easily flag to the operator

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the communication of harmful content by the service,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the failure of the operator to address situations in which a user of the service expresses, on the service, a suicidal ideation, an intention to self-harm or an intention to commit an act that could cause death or serious bodily harm to an individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the fact that the service engaged in a type of harmful behaviour referred to in section 53; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notify the user of its receipt of the flag.

Subsection.Text: The operator of a regulated chatbot service must make a resource person available to users of the service to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: receive usersâ concerns with respect to the communication of harmful content by the service, the fact that the service engaged in a type of harmful behaviour referred to in section 53 or the measures that the operator implements to comply with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: direct users to internal and external resources to address their concerns, such as an internal complaints mechanism or the Commission; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide guidance to users with respect to those internal resources.

Subsection.Text: The operator must ensure that the resource person is easily identifiable and that their contact information is easily accessible to users of the service.

The operator of a regulated chatbot service must submit a digital safety plan to the Commission in respect of each regulated chatbot service that it operates. The digital safety plan must include the following information in respect of the period provided for in the regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with sections 49 to 54, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the risk that harmful content will be communicated by the service and of the risk that the service will engage in any type of harmful behaviour referred to in section 53,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the measures that the operator implements to mitigate those risks,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the effectiveness of the measures, both individually and collectively, in mitigating those risks, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the indicators that the operator uses to assess the effectiveness of the measures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with section 21, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the design features that the operator integrates into the service under that section,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the effectiveness of the measures, both individually and collectively, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the indicators that the operator uses to assess the effectiveness of the measures;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with subsection 22(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with the regulations made under paragraph 126(1)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting any measures that the operator implements to protect children, other than those that it implements to fulfill its obligations under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the criteria and processes, if any, that the operator applies to determine if the Royal Canadian Mounted Police â or another law enforcement agency â should be notified of the existence on the service of content that gives rise to reasonable grounds to suspect that there is a risk that an individual will commit an act that would cause death or serious bodily harm to another individual;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if any law enforcement agencies were notified of the existence on the service of content described in paragraph (f), information respecting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of such notifications,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the name of each law enforcement agency notified, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which each was notified;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the resources, including human resources, that the operator allocates in order to comply with sections 21 to 23 and 49 to 57;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the number of times that the operator received a flag referred to in paragraph 56(a), including the number of flags relating to each type of harmful content communicated by the service and each type of harmful behaviour referred to in section 53 engaged in by the service,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the manner in which the operator triaged and assessed the flags, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: measures taken by the operator with respect to the flags;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the concerns received by the resource person referred to in subsection 57(1) and the internal and external resources to which the resource person directed users for the purposes of that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the topics, and a summary of the findings, conclusions or recommendations, of any research conducted by or on behalf of the operator with respect to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: harmful content communicated by the service,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: harmful behaviour referred to in section 53 engaged in by the service,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: content communicated by the service that poses a risk of significant psychological or physical harm, other than harmful content,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: harmful behaviour engaged in by the service that poses a risk of significant psychological or physical harm, other than harmful behaviour referred to in section 53, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: design features of the service that pose a risk of significant psychological or physical harm;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the measures implemented by the operator for the purposes of complying with its duties with respect to the service under An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: an inventory of all electronic data, other than content communicated during an interaction between the service and a user of the service, that was used to prepare the information referred to in paragraphs (a) to (j) and (n); and

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: any other information provided for by regulations.

Subsection.Text: The operator must ensure that the digital safety plan does not contain any personal information.

For greater certainty, the digital safety plan must not contain any information that must not be disclosed under section 5 of An Act respecting the mandatory reporting of Internet child sexual abuse and exploitation material by persons who provide an Internet service.
The operator must make the digital safety plan publicly available on the service to which the plan relates in an accessible and easy-to-read format.
The digital safety plan that the operator makes publicly available must not contain

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any information about the circumstances in which a law enforcement agency was notified about the existence on the operatorâs service of content described in paragraph (1)(f); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other information regarding the notification of a law enforcement agency about the existence of such content on the operatorâs service, if disclosure of the information could prejudice a criminal investigation, whether or not a criminal investigation has begun.

Subsection.Text: The operator is not required to include any of the following information in the digital safety plan that it makes publicly available:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the inventory of electronic data referred to in paragraph (1)(m);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that is a trade secret; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: financial, commercial, scientific or technical information that is confidential and that is treated consistently in a confidential manner by the person to whose business or affairs it relates.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 4
Heading.TitleText: Regulated Online Services â Duty to Be Transparent

Subsection.Text: The operator of a regulated online service must submit a digital safety plan to the Commission in respect of each regulated online service that it operates. The digital safety plan must include the following information in respect of the period provided for in the regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with section 21, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the design features that the operator integrates into the service under that section,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the operatorâs assessment of the effectiveness of the measures, both individually and collectively, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of the indicators that the operator uses to assess the effectiveness of the measures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with subsection 22(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the manner in which the operator complies with the regulations made under paragraph 126(1)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting any measures that the operator implements to protect children, other than those that it implements to fulfill its obligations under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: information respecting the criteria and processes, if any, that the operator applies to determine if the Royal Canadian Mounted Police â or another law enforcement agency â should be notified of the existence on the service of content that gives rise to reasonable grounds to suspect that there is a risk that an individual will commit an act that would cause death or serious bodily harm to another individual;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
